question_text: A student uses a convex lens with a focal length of 20 cm to form an image of an object placed 30 cm from the lens. Using the lens formula (1/f = 1/u + 1/v), calculate the image distance and determine whether the image is real or virtual, enlarged or diminished.
correct_answer: Image distance (v) = 60 cm. The image is real, inverted, and enlarged (magnification = 2).
explanation: Using 1/f = 1/u + 1/v: 1/20 = 1/30 + 1/v. Solving: 1/v = 1/20 - 1/30 = 3/60 - 2/60 = 1/60, so v = 60 cm. Since v is positive, the image is real. Magnification m = v/u = 60/30 = 2, so the image is twice the size (enlarged).
